results.description: Agency needs clarification on date/location

Agency asks for DR/case number if known

analysis.summary: LAPD Records Division needs additional details (incident date, DR/case number, and location) to locate responsive records and proceed.

draft.subject: RE: RE: Records Request - Clarification Needed
draft.body_text:
Hello LAPD Records Division,

Here are the details you requested:

1. Incident date: March 15, 2024
2. DR/case number: I do not have the DR number or case number at this time.
3. Location: 500 N Main St, Los Angeles, CA (near the intersection of N Main St and W Temple St)

If it helps to narrow the search, please start with any incident/arrest report and associated CAD/dispatch records for this location on 3/15/2024 involving David Lee. If multiple incidents match, please let me know what additional details (time of day, type of incident, etc.) would help narrow it further.

Thank you.

Requester
draft.body_html:
Hello LAPD Records Division,

Here are the details you requested:

1. Incident date: March 15, 2024
2. DR/case number: I do not have the DR number or case number at this time.
3. Location: 500 N Main St, Los Angeles, CA (near the intersection of N Main St and W Temple St)

If it helps to narrow the search, please start with any incident/arrest report and associated CAD/dispatch records for this location on 3/15/2024 involving David Lee. If multiple incidents match, please let me know what additional details (time of day, type of incident, etc.) would help narrow it further.

Thank you.

Requester

Thanks for checking. Please include both the body-worn camera footage and any available dashcam footage for the incident(s) involving Eva Martinez.

If pulling both will significantly delay processing, please proceed with the body-worn camera footage first and let me know the estimated timeframe (or any added cost) to provide the dashcam footage as well.

Thank you.

Requester

Thanks for checking. Please include both the body-worn camera footage and any available dashcam footage for the incident(s) involving Eva Martinez.

If pulling both will significantly delay processing, please proceed with the body-worn camera footage first and let me know the estimated timeframe (or any added cost) to provide the dashcam footage as well.

draft.subject: RE: Fee Acceptance - Frank Wilson
draft.body_text:
Hello,

Thanks for the estimate. I confirm I accept the $35.00 fee to proceed with this request.

What payment methods do you accept (check, money order, or credit card)? If payment must be mailed, please send an invoice and the correct mailing address and any details needed to ensure it’s credited to my request.

I appreciate your help.

Thank you,

Requester
draft.body_html:
Hello,

Thanks for the estimate. I confirm I accept the $35.00 fee to proceed with this request.

What payment methods do you accept (check, money order, or credit card)? If payment must be mailed, please send an invoice and the correct mailing address and any details needed to ensure it’s credited to my request.

I appreciate your help.

Thank you,

Requester

results.description: High fee quote requiring breakdown request

key_points: Estimated total cost is $750.00
Hourly rate quoted: $75/hr
Estimated hours: 9 total (search, review, redaction)
Includes $75 media copying charge
Agency asks to confirm whether to proceed
analysis.summary: NYPD provided a $750 cost estimate with a detailed breakdown and requests confirmation to proceed before continuing.

draft.subject: RE: Fee Response - Grace Kim
draft.body_text:
Thanks for the update on case “Grace Kim” and the quoted $750. To help reduce the cost and turnaround time, we’re happy to narrow the request to just the most critical footage:

- Video from the primary responding officer(s) and the arresting officer(s) only (no other units)
- A tighter time window: 30 minutes before through 60 minutes after the incident/arrest (or whatever minimum window you recommend)

If an incident number, arrest number, complaint number, or related report number would help you identify the correct officers and videos, please let us know what you need and we’ll provide it.

We’re also requesting a waiver or reduction of fees in the public interest as a documentary/news media project, under New York FOIL (Public Officers Law, Article 6).

Thank you,

Requester

draft.subject: RE: RE: Records Request - Sensitive Content Notice
draft.body_text:
Yes, please proceed with the redacted version of the body-worn camera footage, with faces/voices and any identifying details about the juvenile obscured.

Before you process it, could you please confirm:
- Any estimated cost/fees for the redactions and a rough time estimate
- The delivery format (download link is fine)

Also, to help you locate the correct incident, please let me know what incident date, general location, or case number you have associated with the responsive records. If there are multiple related events, I’m happy to narrow the request to a specific report number or date range.

Thank you,

Requester
draft.body_html:
Yes, please proceed with the redacted version of the body-worn camera footage, with faces/voices and any identifying details about the juvenile obscured.

Before you process it, could you please confirm:
- Any estimated cost/fees for the redactions and a rough time estimate
- The delivery format (download link is fine)

Also, to help you locate the correct incident, please let me know what incident date, general location, or case number you have associated with the responsive records. If there are multiple related events, I’m happy to narrow the request to a specific report number or date range.

Thank you,

Requester

results.description: First follow-up after no response (7 days)

draft.subject: Follow-up: Public Records Request - Mike Nelson
draft.body_text:
Hello,

I’m following up on my public information request sent to the Austin Police Department on 2026-01-13 regarding “Mike Nelson.” I know your team may be handling a high volume of requests, so I wanted to check in and see if there’s an update on the status and any anticipated timeline for a response.

We remain interested in obtaining the requested records. If it would help expedite processing, I’m also happy to discuss narrowing or clarifying the scope.

Thank you for your time, and I appreciate your help.

Requester

results.description: Second follow-up after no response (14 days)

draft.subject: Follow-up: Public Records Request - Nancy Green
draft.body_text:
Hello,

I’m following up on my Texas Public Information Act request sent on 2026-01-06 regarding records related to Nancy Green, submitted to the San Antonio Police Department. I have not yet received an acknowledgment, determination, or estimated completion date.

Texas provides a 10-business-day timeframe for an agency to respond or request an applicable extension. Given the time that has passed, please provide a status update and an ETA for when I can expect a response and any available records.

I remain interested in receiving the requested records. If the request needs clarification or narrowing to facilitate processing, please let me know what adjustments would be most helpful.

Thank you.

Requester

results.description: Final follow-up before escalation (21 days)

draft.subject: Follow-up: Public Records Request - Oscar Brown
draft.body_text:
Hello Fort Worth Police Department Public Information Office,

This is my final follow-up regarding my public records request concerning Oscar Brown, originally submitted on 2025-12-31. I’m still awaiting a response.

Under the Texas Public Information Act, agencies generally have 10 business days to respond. Please provide a status update and an estimated date for when I can expect the responsive records (or a written determination regarding the request).

If any part of my request is unclear or too broad, I’m happy to discuss narrowing the scope so it can be processed more efficiently. If needed, please also share the appropriate supervisor or alternate contact who can assist in resolving the status of this request.

Thank you for your help, and I remain interested in receiving the requested records.

Requester

draft.subject: RE: Fee Response - Robert Stone
draft.body_text:
Thanks for the update on the Robert Stone request and the $125.00 estimate.

To help reduce the cost, can we narrow the scope to only:
- Body-worn and/or dash camera video from the primary responding officer(s) and the arresting officer(s) only
- A tighter time window: 30 minutes before through 30 minutes after the incident/arrest (or the shortest window you recommend)

If it would help you identify the correct footage, please let me know what details you need (for example, incident number, report number, CAD event number, exact location, and date/time). I can provide what I have.

Also, if available, please consider a fee waiver or reduction under the Pennsylvania Right-to-Know Law given the public/media interest and our documentary use. If you can share a revised estimate based on the narrowed scope, that would be great.

Thank you,

Requester
